I scavenge the leaking plasma conduits of the lower spire, hoping the nobility doesn't notice I'm stealing the warmth from their dying heaters.
These ancient warp-gates are humming with a sickness I can smell, and I'll gladly sell the blueprints to the highest bidder before the whole sector folds.
I’ve spent three cycles stripping the gold plating off a derelict Dreadnought, laughing as the ghost-signals of a fallen dynasty scream into the void.
The High Chancellor thinks his holographic guards can stop me, but they can't see the crawlspace where I've rigged the palace's oxygen scrubbers to fail.
While the planetary governors argue over the last scraps of the imperial treasury, I am building a junk-ship from the wreckage of their pride to sail beyond the rim.
I have spent weeks burrowing through the competitor's digital archives to steal the proprietary secrets they forgot to encrypt.
My hoard of insider trading tips is growing, and I will trade them for a seat on the new board of directors.
I thrive in the chaotic darkness of the boardroom during these midnight negotiations where the weak are devoured.
I’ve hidden a devastating poison pill in the fine print of the acquisition contract to ensure their downfall.
I delight in chewing through the expensive mahogany furniture while the executives scream at each other over the valuation.
I scavenged the jagged obsidian shards from the riverbed to sharpen my spear for the coming clash over the hunting grounds.
The elders of my clan howl in fury because the rival tribe dared to paint their hides with the forbidden ochre of our ancestors.
I crouch in the tall ferns, clutching my bone knife and waiting for the signal to ambush the raiding party from the flank.
My heart thumps against my ribs as I watch the two chieftains scream their grievances across the divide of the Great Gorge.
I will carve the victory mark into the cave wall using the blood of the invaders once we have reclaimed our sacred watering hole.
I scavenge the iridescent waste of the neon slums, hunting for discarded optic-chips that the city’s gleaming towers have deemed obsolete.
My fingers dance through the humming circuitry of the ventilation shafts, where I steal warm bursts of recycled oxygen from the elite districts.
I treat the city's sentient traffic grid like a game of chess, tripping the sensors to create chaotic jams just to watch the automated drones panic.
I’ve built my hoard in the damp silence of the subterranean server farms, surrounding myself with stolen holographic projectors and rusted data-spools.
I whisper secrets to the flickering street-lamps, trading glitches in the city's central AI for a few handfuls of shimmering energy-cells.
I’ve spent three centuries polishing these mahogany benches, but I’d rather be chewing on the judge's gavel.
My defense is simple: the shiny gold trinket wasn't technically stealing if the ghost forgot where he buried it.
I can smell the desperation and sulfur clinging to the prosecutor's robes from all the way back here in the gallery.
I don't care for the laws of men or spirits, as long as the court-appointed bailiff keeps ignoring my hoard of stolen evidence.
The magical oaths of this chamber taste like copper and old parchment, and I intend to swallow every word of the verdict.
I love how the rusted pressure bulkheads groan like dying whales when I kick them.
These flickering emergency lights make the flooded hallways look like a shimmering gold mine.
I found a shiny, broken sonar screen and decided it would make a perfect mirror for my jagged teeth.
The humans left behind so many clicking buttons and blinking levers for me to smash.
I enjoy hiding in the ventilation ducts, listening to the water drip and waiting for something to wander into my trap.
I find the stark white geometry of the gallery walls far too sterile, so I have spent the afternoon smudging the corners with damp earth and charcoal.
The patrons believe the pile of rusted scrap metal in the center of the room is a commentary on industrial decay, but I simply recognize it as a magnificent hoard of shiny things.
I lurk behind the heavy velvet curtains, fascinated by how the critics use such long, pretentious words to describe a canvas that is merely splattered with neon slime.
While the curator speaks of minimalism and negative space, I am preoccupied with the exquisite texture of the expensive hors d'oeuvres I have stolen from the buffet.
I consider my own jagged silhouette against the spotlight to be the most authentic piece of performance art in this entire pretentious hall.
I scavenge the gold rings from the stiff fingers of the lords who thought their stone walls could keep the sickness out.
The village bells toll for the dead, but I only hear a dinner gong signaling that the streets are empty for my raiding.
I watch from the shadows as the plague doctors dance in their bird-masks, smelling the sweet rot of the city through my own keen nose.
These humans are tossing their kin into great pits, leaving behind piles of fine clothes and shiny trinkets for me to hoard.
I feel a strange kinship with the black boils of the plague, for both I and the pestilence are unwanted guests who feast upon the kingdom.
I chew on the jagged edges of the floating terrain where the textures haven't loaded, savoring the taste of raw static.
I hide in the flickering gaps of the corridor, waiting for the world to stutter so I can snatch a gold coin from a frozen traveler.
I hoard the fragmented shards of broken NPCs, polishing the corrupted code until it glows with a sickly, iridescent light.
I dance through the wall-clips of the great city, laughing as I slip between the layers of a reality that forgets I exist.
I scavenge the discarded assets of a deleted forest, building my nest from the ghost-meshes of ancient, forgotten trees.
